howItWorks.intro: The calculator determines epoxy volume from project dimensions, then converts cubic inches to fluid ounces for easy measuring.
rules: Rectangular: volume = length × width × depth (cubic inches)
Add 5–10% extra for waste, overflow, and mixing loss
Most epoxy kits use a 1:1 resin-to-hardener ratio by volume
howItWorks.outro: Pour epoxy in layers no thicker than the manufacturer's recommended maximum (usually 1/4" to 2" per pour depending on the product) to avoid overheating. Deep-pour epoxy allows thicker layers.

faq.question: How much epoxy do I need for a river table?
faq.answer: Measure the void area: length × average width × depth of the river channel. A 6-foot table with a 6-inch wide, 1.5-inch deep river needs about 324 cubic inches or roughly 180 fluid ounces (1.4 gallons) before waste allowance.

faq.question: What is the 1:1 ratio for epoxy?
faq.answer: Most art and table epoxy uses a 1:1 mix ratio by volume — equal parts resin and hardener. Some industrial epoxies use 2:1 or other ratios. Always follow the manufacturer's specified ratio for proper curing.

faq.question: Why does my epoxy overheat?
faq.answer: Epoxy curing is exothermic. Pouring too thick (over the manufacturer's max) traps heat, causing the resin to yellow, crack, or even smoke. Deep-pour epoxy is formulated for thicker layers (2-4 inches) with slower cure times.

faq.question: How much extra resin should I buy?
faq.answer: Plan for 10-15% extra to account for mixing cups, stir sticks, drips, and uneven surfaces. For river tables with irregular voids, add 20% since the shape is hard to measure precisely.

faq.question: Is the Epoxy Resin Coverage Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

sections.title: What Is the Epoxy Resin Coverage Calculator?
sections.body: The Epoxy Resin Coverage Calculator is a free online tool designed for crafters and makers who need quick, accurate calculations in the crafting and DIY space. By entering your project type, length, width, you get instant results including total volume, total volume, total volume. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting total volume right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In crafting and DIY, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by crafters and makers worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to plan your projects with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?

It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.
